items.headline: How Apple’s big lawsuit could disrupt OpenAI’s IPO plans
items.summary: Apple filed a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI last Friday, and it’s not messing around. The complaint alleges a pattern of misconduct reaching all the way up to OpenAI’s chief hardware officer and claims more than 400 former Apple…

items.headline: San Francisco orders Apple, Google to remove nudify apps from app stores
items.summary: Official estimates Google and Apple likely made millions in nudify app fees.

items.headline: Patreon stops asking AI bots not to scrape — and starts blocking them
items.summary: Patreon is strengthening its defenses against AI scraping by working with Cloudflare to block bots that train AI models on creators’ content without permission. The move marks a shift away from relying on websites using robots.txt alone to…

items.headline: The Download: perimenopause misinformation and China’s latest AI leap

items.headline: Why the first GPU financiers are turning to inference chips in a $400 million deal
items.summary: A $400 million chip-backed loan points to the next wave of AI infrastructure deals.

items.headline: A scorecard for the AI age
items.summary: Sarah Friar, CFO of OpenaAI, introduces a practical AI scorecard to measure ROI through useful work, cost per successful task, dependability, and return on compute.
